title: betula papyrifera subsp. neoalaskana
intro: Betula papyrifera subsp. neoalaskana, also known as paper birch, is a subspecies of the paper birch tree, which is native to North America and is characterized by its distinctive white, papery bark. The medicinal properties of Betula papyrifera subsp. neoalaskana include its ability to reduce inflammation and its antiseptic and antibacterial properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to lower blood pressure, reduce stress and anxiety, and improve digestion, as well as its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. Herbal preparations of Betula papyrifera subsp. neoalaskana include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
